title: Slide Publisher — Storytelling Style
description: A named storytelling configuration. Determines which narrative arcs, beat allocations, layout-intent priors, density rules, and rhetorical posture the compiler applies. Users pick a style at compile time, override keys, or author their own.

style_version.description: Semver of the style schema. v0.2 styles emit '1.0.0'.

name.description: Human-readable style name. Shown in the wizard.

description.description: What the style optimizes for, when to use it, what it sacrifices.

authority.description: Who or what the style derives from (e.g., 'Joseph Campbell (Hero's Journey)'). Optional.

arc_set.description: Arcs the compiler may use. First entry is the default unless `default_arc` is set.

default_arc.description: Default arc when the user doesn't specify one. Must be in arc_set.

beats.description: Per-beat allocation rules. Keys are IR beat names; values configure min/max count + allowed layout intents.

slides_per_minute.description: Target slide count = duration_min × slides_per_minute. Reynolds ~0.5; McKinsey ~1.5; Tufte ~1.5.

max_body_blocks_per_slide.description: Compiler refuses to allocate more body_blocks than this per slide.

prefer_visual.description: When true, body block selection biases toward image_placeholder/diagram_placeholder over prose/bullets.

prefer_quote_over_metric.description: Tie-breaker when both quote and metric block kinds are candidates.

posture.description: Rhetorical posture. Affects voice_notes the compiler suggests.

evidence_weighting.description: Tilt of evidence selection when the IR's evidence_anchor is 'hybrid'.

voice_notes.description: Guidance the compiler surfaces to the user about voice + word choice.

extends.description: Optional name of a parent style to inherit from. Style file's own keys override parent keys.
